title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Environmental Protection Agency relating to "Decabromodiphenyl Ether and Phenol, Isopropylated Phosphate (3:1); Revision to the Regulation of Persistent, Bioaccumulative, and Toxic Chemicals Under the Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA)".
plain_summary: This bill stops a new Environmental Protection Agency rule from taking effect. The EPA rule was about controlling two dangerous chemicals that can build up in people and the environment and cause harm. The rule would have required workers to wear protective equipment when working with one of these chemicals and made other safety changes. Congress is using its power to reject this rule, so the new safety requirements will not happen.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress does not approve of a new rule made by the Environmental Protection Agency. This rule was about controlling two specific chemicals called Decabromodiphenyl Ether and Phenol, Isopropylated Phosphate. These chemicals can stay in the environment for a long time, build up in living things, and cause harm. The rule was part of changes to how the government controls dangerous chemicals under a law called the Toxic Substances Control Act. The Environmental Protection Agency published this rule in the Federal Register on November nineteenth, twenty twenty four. Because Congress disapproves of this rule, it will not become law and will have no power.
